content.question: How much of the observed increase in SES achievement gaps in Finland after the 1990s reflects reactivation of within-school selection (class differentiation, language specialization) versus residential segregation, and does the ratio of these two mechanisms differ significantly in urban (high-segregation) versus rural settings?
content.reduction_path: Decompose Finnish PISA and longitudinal achievement data by school type (urban vs. rural), class-level assignment mechanism (tracked vs. mixed), and residential background, using instrumental-variable or regression-discontinuity design to isolate the effect of within-school class-selection versus residential sorting on SES gaps.
